There is not a part that isn't meant to go in the mouth.My only complaint is the handles are connected to the middle however in the product image it looks open.  The gaps between the Soothie part of the teether and the handles are miniscule making it hard for my 3 month old son to grasp this teether.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I use these washcloths with the mustela cleansing fluid instead of wipes, and to cover the little guy down there when I'm changing his diaper (to block a potential waterfall). I also use these to cover baby's belly during a bath to keep him warm, and they are thin and soft enough to get into his skin folds without bothering baby. Really versatile.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I got this to go in my Mia Moda stroller, and it would not stay in place. It kept sliding down the straps.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Purchased these because I liked the fact that you can heat them or freeze them.  I'm a D cup and they fit very well.  Comfy and retains the heat nicely.  I heat mine up in the microwave for 1 min 30 seconds and place on my breasts prior to pumping.  It did help unclog my ducts!  Only downfall is that one side of the pads is pretty thin so you have to be careful of tears.  Mine already has some micro holes in it.Definitely recommend for breastfeeding mamas!

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: they must have altered the design at some point because what i received isn't a flip open spout, it's a very annoying bend open spout that opens over the edge.  Big Hassle to use :( DIdn't realize it wasn't what i ordered until i had already opened it.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Very thin for the price.  It is very hard to get on the mat.  I will have to see how it wears.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I don't have a lot to compare this to, but I do feel like it is a nice large size, yet still fits on the dresser top, and it is very soft and plush! Our son likes to lay here and kick and babble, so I feel like he is definitely comfortable on it. We bought the mink dot covers also on Amazon, and they fit great!

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I bought this swing because I wanted something I could use anywhere, such as in the yard or on a picnic etc. It does the trick, only my son doesn't like it. Go figure...Pros:Very compact. This swing will fold up ultra compact and fit into small storage areas. Let's put it this way. I have a regular sliding door closet with 2 bouncers, a walker and 3 storage totes and this thing slid right in next to the folded walker. I have a hatchback Toyota Yaris and this thing fits in the car with ease.Long Battery Life. I have used the same batteries now for 5 months and they are still going strong. The motor and music has a timer on it which allows you to determine when it wil stop. And, conveniently, lets say you forget to turn it off after you grab your baby, it will turn off after awhile regardless if you set the timer or not.Sturdy design. My 2 year old toddler loves to play with this thing when I'm not looking. He will pull it out of the closet, set it up, turn it on and sit in it. Fortunately it can take a few beatings like that. He's too heavy for it and obviously doesn't make sure its set up correcty before plopping himself in it but the little swing can handle that every once in awhile.Cons:My cons are all personal really. Nothing releated to the design. My youngest simply doesn't like it. Nothing that the manufacturer could have changed to prevent that. He just wants to be held instead:)

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I do not recommend this. I normally love all things oxo, but this is a huge fail. When we first got this, the soft plastic top that opens up was so hard that my 14 month old son could not get his hand through on his own to grab his o's. now almost a year later, the plastic is permanently open and o's fall out everywhere when this tips over. Super annoying. The munchkin snack trap seems like a much better product for this.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Should have paid attention to the product details. The suction cups only work for smooth surfaces. My baby bathtub has very fine textured surface but the mat did not stick at all. I ended up getting Slip-X tub tattoos and they work well even though they also specify that they're only good on smooth surfaces.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I was in a desperate bind after my son woke up one day and decided to start eating the crib rails on his Babyletto Modo crib. I needed something and fast, before he destroyed all the rails with his little teeth. Apparently there aren't many options in crib rail covers despite the fact that I assume the majority of babies go through this destructive chewing stage. The only options it seemed were the Leachco brand and this one, or make it myself, which was going to take a lot longer. So far these have been doing the trick. My son chews on them instead of the rails, which is the main benefit. However, I wish they were a tad bigger in all directions. The crib has narrow rails, but it still isn't narrow enough for this to cover completely. The other option seemed like it would hang down way too low. If they had one in between these two sizes I think it would be much better. Also, my son likes to hang out at the corners of the crib, so if these were slightly longer, I'd feel better that he couldn't totally attack the corners with his teeth. The ties could also be longer. As it is, they fit, but just barely. The main negative is that these stink, literally. It's either from the packaging or the materials, but there's a distinct chemical odor and after washing a few times, it's still there. I feel terrible that my son is chewing on these things despite the chemicals, but until I have the time to sit down and make my own, these have to suffice. I also feel that it's kind of expensive for just one rail cover, and because of the crib we have, we need 4 of these covers instead of 3.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I found this together set at Wal-Mart for $8. I'd be willing to pay a LITTLE extra to avoid that store, but SIX dollars? Come on Amazon.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: we dropped both parts numerous times, and it still works fine. no pan/tilt/zoom, and you will not be able to mount the camera part, but otherwise does what it claims.  I like the feature that wakes it up when there is noise near the camera.  Would be nice if the volume could be turned higher, as I can often sleep right through the baby crying.  Luckily my wife wakes up to it.  I think there are probably all-around options out there, but we were happy with this, and it does the trick.  Would also have been nice to be able to add additional cameras/viewers, i.e. if it worked as a system.  Also, no wifi or internet capability, so buyers must know the limitations.  I suggest researching other options before picking any one of these monitors.  Consumer Reports has a good breakdown.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I thought this was a crafty device, but honestly, I'm not sure if it gets hot enough to do any good, and the product manual has pretty stringent usage guidelines, so I'm a bit iffy about keeping things in there longer in order to make the hotter. In addition, another thing I didn't think to find out about is if its BPA free, and I don't think it is, because I don't remember seeing anything about the product saying it is. I just boil my stuff now, and I kinda wish I saved the money, I could have bought a case of name brand diapers or a canister of formula for the price.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Expected these to be a little bigger they're more of a nose rag than a wash cloth. Besides that it's pretty good doesn't reel or fall apart

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: It does what it says...but I don't think it's all that necessary.  You could get the same result by using a colander and letting water run over that.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I wish it was a bit taller and was cheaper. I love the look but found it expensive for what you get. I found a cute chevron pink and green lamp shade at target and a cute white base and it matches the color of the Dena Happi collection perfectly. I returned this one and bought 2 at Target of the other lamps. I think it looks better.  I love the look of the lamp but for the price it is not worth it.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: it's a nice toy, easy to attach everywhere, easy to arrange near/far low/high thanks to the flexible arch, great...I'm giving it only 3 stars because it seems since the original creation of this toy, tiny love brand have decided to cut a bit on the creation budget and have removed some little details, as the fleece on the wheel, the little colored accents on the sun, the textured fabric on the flower...etc...so, I do recommend it, but don't be exited by the great details...you won't have them...

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: You get what you pay for with these blankets. They are too small for swaddling, but they ended up working well for burp towels. They also didn't stay soft after washing. My baby is now 3 months old and these are already too small to even lay over top of him in the stroller. If you're looking for a thin summer swaddle, go with the Aden and Anais blankets. They are large and stretchy...perfect for swaddling. They are also great for a thin breathable blanket to go over a car seat, stroller, or a breastfeeding baby. They are a bit more expensive, but I purchased one pack of 4 and they are still holding up well after numerous washes.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: It's a very neat, high contrast mobile with a lot of different image options but if you have a modern styled crib with a thicker top slat then this thing will not fit.  Only disappointed they didn't make this item versatile enough for different crib designs.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Out of the 4 rattles only 2 are rattles and the other 2 are just stuffed bugs. The rattlers are not noisy enought. My daughter doesn't even hear them herself while she moves her arms.The other issue is that the socks are not elastic enough and too big for ther feet. Just as she starts to move they will come off.My daughter was 4 month old when i bought this.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: pediatrician told us that our baby's head (lower side of the head) is a bit flat so I immediately went online to search for a good pillow. I came across this product and purchased right away because it had good ratings/comments. My baby's been sleeping on this for 2 weeks now. I don't really see any difference in his head shape (It may take some time, who knows). I wish the neck area had more cushioning. But using this is better than sleeping on a flat surface. Overall, I think it's an OKAY product so I'm giving 3 stars.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I love the idea of a parent organizer - a place to put a water bottle, cell phone, keys, etc. However, when the UPPAbaby Cruz stroller is in forward facing position, you cannot recline the seat if the parent organizer is strapped on! It gets in the way and the seat will not recline. What use is the organizer if I have to unstrap it in order to recline the seat? I will definitely be returning this item to amazon since it is pretty much useless. Don't waste your time and money on this one.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: it seals in the diaper smell well, as the diaper pail fills up, you'll be able to smell it, so this requires somewhat frequent changes.  The refills arent too cheap and don't last very long, so that's a bit of a bummer, as it's turning into a quarterly expense for us, but it does perform as expected.I didn't give it five stars, as i didn't feel the plastic constructon was that great.  it seems easy for the hinges to get stuck or diapers that dont fall all the way through, and require a bit of a push to get it through.still, very good and I would still recommend it.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Maybe it's that I live in earthquake country and my house is in no way level in any direction but I can get this adjusted so the sel close mech works and five minutes later it is back to being a non self close gate.  Other than that, it's a decent baby gate but I imagine if you are buying this it's for the self closing...

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: If ur like me you think "oh, so a couple people just got bum models. Mine wont be." Well, maybe you will be one of the lucky ones but if you do open ur box and find the pump to be so weak after 5 minutes of pumping u get a couple drops, this item is NOT returnable! The sticker on the box when you open it says so. I still plan however on calling the manufacturer to raise hell. i will update this if anything changes. But for me, i was looking for a pump to use for making my own baby food when a recipe just calls for a tablespoon here or there or for pumping at night now that my infant is sleeping thru so i would not have to drag out electric pump. Maybe if your engorged it will work... so disappointed...

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: These are horrible. The moment my daughter drops it the contents spill out. Do not buy these. Waste of money.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: great product, works great, bought 4. kids have broken 2 in the last few months. Wish it was stronger just so the older kids could use them without accidently breaking them. Purchased the optional catches since by molding is more rounded.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: These are absolutely perfect for making, storing, and freezing homemade baby food!  They stack perfectly, and the size is awesome for little tummies.  The only problem is that they shatter very easily if they fall onto the floor from the freezer.  I've had to throw a few away because of it...

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: This gave our son diaper rash when used without water, so now we use a squirt of this and a squirt of water and it works fine for the super-big messes. For most messes water alone works just as well.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: These bottles are OK. I ordered them because they were the biggest bottles I could find for my 1 year old son. The air venting system isn't the greatest, lots of bubbles.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I normally love the product but half the wipes in the box were the thick and textured version and the other were the thin plain ones. Did I get a defective box? I had to use them anyways since it was this or paper towels in the house to use on my baby.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I do not recommend this product. The sticky Velcro did not stay attached to my brick fireplace and my 8 month old son was easily able to pull it off. I threw this away about 2 days after I got it.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: We tried many different cups and this is the one my son loves, it's great easy to clean and all that good stuff. However, I have had to replace so many of these cups because they crack when dropped. I wish they were made out of a stronger material.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Bought this over 2 years ago , so I cant' comment on the pricing now. We got it as a set and at a good deal,It survived my daughter climbing it, doing pull-ups on it, and learning to open and slam a drawer. The only thing it didnt' last was sub-standard movers. Paint job took some serious blows before it started to chip. I like the drawer and I like DaVinci items in general.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: We bought 6 size ones with the snaps and we love them.  Our little guy is almost 21 pounds now (7 months) and still fits them on the second to largest setting (a little snug), so we still have a few pounds to go before we need the size 2.The covers work great with the infant size pre-fold diapers.  In seven months only one explosion in these has occurred, while countless disposable incidents have happened.  We get the dark spots out of the white trim by occasionally using a cotton swab and a dilute bleach solution, followed by lots of rinsing.Not only are these cute, they work great and 7 months later (at 21 pounds) the covers look new!**Update*** 10 monthsI finally decided that it was time to move to the size 2 duo wraps.  The size ones lasted until our little guy was ~23 lbs and 30"

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: It does it's job if it's not falling off of the faucet. We have a garden tub that the kids like to use and this thing won't stay on there. I only gave it 3 stars because it worked well with our other tub. Apparently it depends on the tub!

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: This is a great 'Babies first rattle!"As baby gets older its very easy to grasp and hold.  I received this as a gift for my daughter and then bought another one for my son.It washes well and has a nice rattle noise.My only concern is that at a young age when babies motor skills are not fully developed - this toy can be dangerous!My son swings it around and sometimes hits himself in the head... so we had to put this away for awhile...

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I bought this for my 8-month daughter before an international flight and hope it will keep her busy. I thought she liked tags, however, the tag on this blanket is too small and she is not interested.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: This pillow helped me during my pregnancy, but I must say it does lose its density rather quickly. After a couple weeks, the pillow was not as full and fluffy as it initially was when I bought it. It also takes up quite a bit of space in the bed (we have a queen size bed)

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: After you wind this up, it plays for less than two minutes before you have to wind it up again.  It is now on my son's crib as viewing pleasure rather than a soothing device.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: We used one of these to keep an end table that doesn't close closed, so our cat wouldn't bang the door of it to get our attention. It didn't stick so I had to super glue it to get it to stay (it just pulled the varnish off and then wouldn't go back) and the super glue worked. Now that it stays it's working perfectly.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: The pictures don't show the type of outlet shape this is meant for so you may end up with the wrong covers.  Although I sent it back I was never given my money back.  Be careful.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I love this little chair. The clamps are adjustable so you can use it on pretty much any table.  It is super lightweight  so it isn't any trouble to carry it along.  I've recommended it to all my friends.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: PROS:--Can be used hot or cold, which not many similar products can do--Reasonably pricedCONS:--Too bulky, which ultimately made it so I didn't use them that much (the gel "Soothies" are MUCH better)

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I used this once since receiving it and this was my experience. I received the package and immediately suctioned it to my bathtub wall to see how it looked, I left it there thinking nothing of it and within 5 minutes heard a crash and went in to find it had fallen. I figured that it may have to do with it being dry and suction cups often work better when wet. Once my child awoke after a nice day at the beach, I proceeded to run the bath and attempted to suction the bath seat to the tub - which did not work out since I have a gripped bottom on my tub to prevent slipping while showering, it is stated that the product needs a smooth surface to suction properly I just didn't think the slight texture would be a problem. I decided to use it anyways, it wasn't impossible though it would work better if it was suctioned and I was able to wash my baby's arms, legs and torso easily. The issues arose when I wanted to wash his bottom, back, neck and hair. The hard structure made this quite difficult to do alone since I had to lift him up and hold him while washing these areas. I will keep it because my baby just loved sitting there splashing and kicking his legs, I guess if he's happy that's what is important. FYI - my son is 23 in long and 12 lb and he fit comfortably in it but I will update on what weight and length he is when he becomes to big for it.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: This is a great tub. It has many awesome features. The infant sling holds a newborn safely and seems to be very comfortable. We have yet to convert it from it's newborn status but it looks as though the "big girl" setting will work just fine. Two features that I LOVE: 1. The temperature gauge, very convenient and safe. 2. The hook to hang from the shower, allows for a thorough dry. Overall, baby is happy so mommy is happy.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I purchased two of these; one for a Pack 'N Play and one for a Baby Bjorn Travel Crib. It was the top choice in the Pack 'N Plays "Customers Who Bought This Item Also Bought" section and had decent reviews so I thought it would work well for both. However, the cotton material is thin and ill fitting for both travel cribs. I can only compare it to trying to put a full size fitted sheet on a queen mattress. You have to really work and stretch this sheet to fit either pad and once you do get it on the sleeping pad folds in on itself because the sheet just wasn't made to stretch that far. After that you have to work the pad to rest flat on both the Pack 'N Play and Baby Bjorn Travel Crib. Once the pad is flat you can see a seem which should be along the edge of the pad, longways, is 3" inside the edge on the pad and the elastic edge of the sheet, longways, which should be under the pad, like a fitted sheet on a mattress, is stretched so far that it is up over the edge of the pad. The only thing this sheet has going for it is the price but you'd really be better off spending the extra money to get something thicker and better fitting.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: After reading the negative reviews of this mobile (and seeing its "flimsiness" for myself at a local Babis R Us store), I went in search of other mobiles with a moon/stars motif.  I found several, but the cutest one by far is the one that matches the Lambs & Ivy "Twinkle Twinkle" bedding set.  It has a white moon and many blue and yellow stars...[...]Hopefully other people will be able to take advantage of this -- I'm not sure how long it will be offered at this price, but good luck!  I was so excited when I found it!

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Everything is useful..the hair brush is not the one in the picture, but I actually like the one I got better than the one shown. The baby nail clipper has a weird safety feature that makes it hard to use and the nasal bulb is useless because it doesn't suck anything at all, it's got a piggy bank type plug in the bulb , hospital one much better

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Before I had my sunshine I did a lot of research on baby wraps, I decided to get the moby. Of course after I had her I then found out that you can buy 5 yards of jersey sheet material (or something like that), and make it yourself with no sewing involved. If I had known there was a material that required no sewing I would of just bought that instead of the moby. I myself am not an artsy crafty girl. AnywaysPros- easy on your back- effective for holding baby close while shopping, cleaning, or whatever task you need done- grows with your infant so you can do more holdsCons- expensive- inconvenient! I really hate that you have to reconfigure it after putting your baby in it twice. Let me tell you when you have a screaming, fussy baby who just wants held, the last thing you want to do is mess with the stupid wrap while putting them down. Also if you are in public the ends drag all over the ground. Its super annoying!- not great for big busted women. My boobs are huge, so this ends up taking a lot more material to wrap around my body and it become hard to tie around the waist for the finishing touches.- INSTRUCTIONS SUCK! Seriously if I relied on the booklet that comes with the moby I would of given up. I looked up youtube videos and it was a lot more informative.Overall I think its a great starting point for a babywearing mamma or dad. I will continue to use it when I have more time to plan and "wrap" it around my body before I go some where, but I'm also getting the ergo baby carrier for the easier on and off routine..

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I just read some of the other reviews that Medela customer service will give them to you for free!!  Wow.... No more buying them!  I am going to tell you some tips about this tubing though...Make sure you use the correct tubing for your pumps b/c they will not work correctly if you use other Medela tubes made for other pumps.  It makes sense but I just thought it wouldn't matter.  Well it kept stopping up my milk ducts so dont do it!Also, for people who have not used these breastpumps yet...These tubings are VERY cheaply made..Reminds me on how some cars spark plugs are meant to crap out on you after 30,000 miles....  These tubes do not seal well.  The plastic applicator that goes into the tube on one end always seems to come loose on me after about 11 times using it and once it comes out just throw it away b/c you dont get good enough suction.  If you're anything like me you'll end up getting stopped milk ducts all the time!  Lots of things I was never told...But I know everyone's boobs are different.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: We bought this for trips to the zoo and parks. The stroller itself is nicely made, it's easy to fold and it stands up when folded which is a nice feature. However, we had to return it. It was good for a few days but the wheels wore out quickly. I can't imagine the issue not occurring with a replacement so we switched to a completely different stroller and have had better luck.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I have to say, I hate this thing.  It's unfortunate that there's only 1 button for power / scanning.  Half the time that I hit the button to scan a temperature, the thing turns off.  When I actually do get it to scan, I get 10 different readings each time I take it.  So it does absolutely nothing for me.Based on all of the high reviews, I can only assume I'm missing something....but simply taking a temperature shouldn't have so much room for error!

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I only used this pad for less than 3 times. size is too small to cover the car seat. It is a waste of money to buy it...

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: If I had seen in the shop, I wouldn't have bought it. My little one wasn't so happy with this backpack. I don't think it is useful as a leash. It is just an immitation. So, if you want a pink dog backback which is roomy (that's why I keep it), buy it! But if you want a leash, try to find something else.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I was under the impression that the monkey would be like a beanie baby... meaning heavier. It's really light and all the baby needs to do is move an arm and it pulls the pacifier out of her mouth. Also, the pacifier is permanently attached to the monkey which makes washing it difficult - either the monkey or the pacifier when it falls on the ground.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: This diaper cover looks really cute, more like little pants than a diaper cover.  But it also functions more like little pants than a diaper cover.  We lanolized it, but it still leaks a lot.  It's also got a very high waist, which makes it difficult for our newborn and his bellybutton.  We're going to use it as our extra cover for the diaper bag.  It works most of the time, but I like the Babee Greens and Loveybums covers better.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: When it was time for me to go back to work, my then-6-week-old son absolutely REFUSED to drink from the Avent bottles we'd chosen. However, we had a Ventaire bottle on hand -- I think it was a shower gift -- and he took to it immediately.That was a mixed blessing. The narrow Ventaire bottle (he would never take the wide one), I believe, led to difficulties with breastfeeding, which we'd previously done well with, and eventually he stopped nursing altogether at 2 1/2 months.I also didn't care for the Ventaire nipples. The first level nipples were so slow that he could barely get any milk at all, while the second level were too fast and led to further nursing issues. (Luckily, just about any standard-size nipples will fit these bottles, so I used the Dr. Brown's ones, with better luck.)However, we had very very few issues with gas, and we had no leaking issues, either, with these bottles. So once nursing was no longer an issue, I was quite happy with the Ventaire ... until the news hit about BPA in plastic bottles. I pitched them then.That said, because of the no-gas feature and the lack of leaking, I would use the Ventaires again IF Playtex came out with a BPA-version, and IF my next kiddo would take the wider version. (I guess we'd still have to find the right nipple, though, as I wasn't happy with the Playtex flow rates.)

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: This is good for the convenience. However once your baby is up to 8oz it is really hard to get the formula in without making a mess.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: This is a nice product, It warms the bottle and seams perfect on the surface. There has been nothing wrong with ours. However, we are usually dealing with frozen breast mik. We found it much easier and faster to heat water in a mug in the microwave. Then remove the hot mug and place the frozen milk in the mug. It doesn't take long for it be at a perfect temperature. You can use milk from the refrigerator as well just don't heat the water as much.Great product, but we didn't really need it.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Nice for travel. Small and cute. But, wish it was a little bit louder. Even at highest volume it wasn't as loud as I would have liked.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I really love these blankets. They are really soft and so far have really kept. The only reason I'm giving it 3 stars is because they aren't that great for doing the baby bundle (burrito). It's an awkward shape and it's hard to do the bundle. But it's great to tuck in my daughter when she's in her car seat and I use it to put over her car seat to block some of the sun.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I really wanted to love this stroller as it could have been perfect for my boys but it just didn't do it for me...Pros:-easy to assemble...I did it myself while 9 months pregnant-the basket underneath is amazing..you can store a ton down there!-did fit through any doors I triedCons:-wheels are terrible, I was always fighting to get over even the slightest crack in sidewalk-not steady, I worried it would tip over, it never did but I NEVER took my hands off of it while the babies were in-flimsy frame-worthless sun shade, it looks amazing but I found that no matter what it was never actually blocking sun-no lock to hold it folded plus it slams down scuffing up the bar and shade.-steering is a nightmare....never plan on talking on the phone or sipping a water bottle while pushing this monsterOverall I was not pleased at all with this stroller, at over two hundred dollars I expected more, would have been better off buying the double handle one no name one that is less than half the price.I ended up buying a baby jogger city mini double and absolutely love it, pricey but worth every penny!

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: It's soft but barely fits our pack n play, it covers but doesn't look like it's made for it necessarily.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: It's useful in the house, but not practical for anything else.  If I wear it out on a walk with the baby, we are both sweating (i tend to be cold, but there really is a lot of fabric!).  I can't use it outside the house because it drags on the floor when you are trying to put it on (which can be difficult by yourself as it is).  But if you are in your clean air-conditioned house, it is useful especially for newborns.  My two month old does not like it unless she is nursing to sleep, but then I always wake her up getting her out!  So, useful but in limited settings.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: This night light is adorable.  It changes color and gives our curious little guy something to focus on while he's trying to fight off sleep.  It seems to help him calm down a bit instead of looking around the room investigating everything- he just watches it change color as he drifts off.  I like that it's portable, too.  I've taken it off the base and set it on the table next to the couch on a particularly rough night when he wouldn't calm down.  As soon as he noticed it he stopped wiggling and eventually fell asleep.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Dream On Me 3" Portable Crib MattressJust arrived today. We are using it in a portable crib at our house for our grand daughter. Support seems to be just right. I would recommend this item.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: This mobile works fine if you want a visual stimulant. There are a lot of colors and shapes to look at. The oversized clip does a good job grabbing onto most anything. The plastic cards are water and tear resistant. I can clip this to my car grab bar above the car seat which works well because it is in the child's line of sight and is too far away to grab.Don't buy this if your child is old enough to be grabbing and pulling on it (or has older siblings that will be doing so). The lower cards have a flimsy attachment to the upper cards and will rip off easily. The overall construction will not stand up to much abuse. This mobile is best for babies 6 months and younger who are still fascinated with visual stimulants and aren't so grabby.Instead of this Wimmer mobile, I would recommend a toy that is both visually AND physically stimulating such as theLamaze Freddie the Fireflywhich is about the same price, can also attach to most anything, and has better durability. Lamaze makes a whole line of such toys which will last through multiple stages of your baby's development.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Perfect for small spaces- Not a lot of space in side compartments - would rather have 3 spaces for diapers but perfect for my needs.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I contacted the manufacturer about the ink coming off their placemats. They said they know there are a lot of those complaints, and they don't believe any of them. Apparently all of their customers seeing the same exact problem are wrong. And they won't answer any questions about what sort of ink they're using and whether it's safe for kids to eat, even though they keep hearing from customers that the ink rubs off on food.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: My daughter is now 3 months old and because I speak so highly of this luv u zoo activity gym, I finally decided to write a review. Our daughter started really paying attention to this at about 3 weeks old. The difference in this gym and other ones is that this has different settings. It can do flashing lights and music or spinning mobile and music. Our daughter still only likes the flashing lights with music. The lights catch her attention. She lays under it for an hour or so at a time. I clip extra toys to it and she LOVES it. She smiles as soon as I put her under it now. This really helped get a few extra minutes to clean the house, etc., especially when she was only a few weeks old. I think this is a definite must have. If you are going to invest in a gym, why get one that cost almost the same and has no lights on it. This one is the way to go!

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I didn't like it, it's not really practical. You can collect a little (almost tiny) amount of milk, it's not a big deal. Maybe it's work in the firsts weeks but now, two months breastfeeding my baby is not the best choice. I regret buying it.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: This stuff works great for baby proofing. Have had it on our coffee tables and other sharp edges for over a year. Still in place and working. Has saved my son from some nasty bumps and bruises many times. Totally worth it. Wish it came in black.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I wish i would of done more research. OH wait...wait...I Did...Yet missed a few "issues" with it....Ok this thing works well. It does exactly what it supposed to do. So i cant give it any less stars because it IS functional.Here is what i HATE about this thing.** I cannot mute the sound! Why not? I have a BRIGHT LED light that tells me how loud the baby is crying (in my case TWO babies in TWO separate rooms). So on the occasional times that i know that my babies are crying and do not want to hear it (but want to be able to still SEE them) i think there should be a mute button.** Because you cannot MUTE this thing. WHY OH WHY do i need a bright LED to tell me how loud they are being? There aren't alot of adults out there (i figure) that are as sensitive to sleep as i am. I wake up to every noise there is and lights bother me. So  i hate the LED lights. It would make perfect sense to have the LED lights if i MUTED the thing and wanted to have a visual of the noise level. I am stuck with a noise and light show. I put electrical tape on the LED to help.** The range isnt that great. It seemed far from the description but no. My babies and I sleep on the third floor. Our hot tub is almost directly below them on the first floor (outside of course..You think that would go without saying but some people.....). When we go down there it peeps saying  its out of range. It flickers really. One moment its in range and the next its not. So much for relaxing in the hot tub.** The battery!! I cannot fathom why the battery doesn't last at least 12 hours? It doesn't even last 8! Now the great people at Motorola didn't think this through. They also didn't make it easy to buy another charger.  So you absolutely need to have it charging at night or at some point you will hear the battery warning peep. Its not enough that you have babies waking you up. But this thing will too. Than after a full 8-9 hours on the charger. You will NEED to either bring the charger where ever you want the monitor or turn it off every time you grab your baby from the crib. But if your exhausted and forget. It will die by 6pm at night (just in time for bedtime). My iphone seems to last all day but not this monitor.**I dropped it once. Yes once....and it broke the camera. Well it broke something inside. My handy dandy husband took it apart and fixed it. So be careful. The monitor seems pretty durable. Ive dropped it a couple times and its still working.I have invested over $400 (two cameras) in this thing. And i wish to....well you know, i would have gone with a security type camera not a baby monitor. Than at least i could make more use out of it when they get older.Sincerely,An exhausted mom of twins.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I love this spout. For the first two weeks.It's great - it doesn't leak, and the baby acclimated to it perfectly.However, after about 2 weeks, it deteriorates quickly. They say you should replace it every 2 months. You probably really shouldn't go for more than 30 days.I keep using these since my baby likes them, but it would be nice if they lasted longer.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: yes, my daughter loves this.  She loves Elmo.I think its okay.  it clicks which I love.  It keeps drinks cold- another plus.  I just don't like all the parts- makes cleaning hard and theres always a missing piece or a piece that never got completely clean.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: The fit can be better but it doesn't leak!For the price, it is pretty clever. It comes in handy when you don't have sippy cups available.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I was disappointed by the "nipple", or spout, on this bottle.  I used them successfully and happily with my other son.  I ordered some for my new baby and we cannot use them.  You have to suck and suck so hard to get any water out.  I have tried it and it is really hard for me to even get much out.  I tried to open the hole a little, but it has not helped.  It may have just been a bad one, because I liked these bottles two years ago for my other son.  I wont risk wasting my money by buying another one though.  I like the Avent one, and it is working well.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: This is a must for baby bath time.  Not sure about over time as I have read feedback stating that it smells after months of use, but right now it's a back and knee saver!

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Product is nice and fluffy.  Doesn't provide any support for baby and the color is not practical especially if you are going to use it to cover resturaunt high chairs.  White just doesn't work!

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: We really like this mattress for our infant and toddler. This mattress is very good for kids with skin allergies. Also this is a solid foam instead of a spring mattress so it is great for kids that bounce or move around a lot in the bed. We hope to get more mileage out of this because there are not springs to wear out.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I bought, tried and store. Its now sitting nicely in my storeroom. A bowl and tablespoon can do the same trick. So consider again before buying...

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: My son has pretty much ignored this toy (he is 11 months).  Because he can get his fingers in the holes he can grip it; he will shake it for a moment then drop it and move on.  It is okay, I would not buy it again.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: What a waste of $20. I don't know what all the hype is about. It's just a tiny squeaky giraffe, I could have gotten any tiny squeaky toy that my baby put in his mouth and called it a teether and sold it for $20.My baby is 4 months old and doesn't care much for it...

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: these suck because they don't suck for long. They worked at first but time has proved, getting a cheaper regular bowl would suffice.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: The chair is overall a good product, but when I've received it the chair was a little bit dirty and have scratches on the back of the chair, also the fixation to hold the baby were not easy to assemble, I had to unsew the strap and resew it...

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: it works very well so far.it was hard to open at first but once I knew how to do it, it was easy.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Would be a great snack cup except my 2 year old can easily take the top off and dump the snacks.  Wish it had a more secure top since it's great sized and good handles for little hands.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Go buy some baby aspirin and bandaids and save yourself the money from this worthless first aid kit. What a waste of money!

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Bought this for Dragoncon for my 1 year old daughter. People commented on how cute she looked. However, for the price I expected it to look less worn after just one wear & wash.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I have used a few baby monitors and have to say that this one is really good primary based on the picture quality and overall build quality of the unit.If you are in the market for baby monitors - you can either get the ones which come with the LED screen or the ones that you can see on your iphone/on the web from anywhere in the world.Personally for a baby monitor - I would prefer the one like this one with a dedicated screen to view the baby since you might be using your iphone/android for phone calls or anything else.Overall I would this unit to be well made and functionally very well recommended

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Works great for my 2 and 3 year olds to wash their hands in the sink. Even with a step stool they couldn't quite reach the faucet. Still leaves enough space in the sink for adults to wash their hands. But slips on and off easily enough if you want to remove it for any reason. Only reason I knocked off a star us because if it or the faucet do get wet it is too slippery to stay on so you have to dry it off first...not really a big deal.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Pros:Large with room for baby to growWarmer than other popular sleep sacksAttractiveButton shoulders allow for easy on and offCons:Quality could be better: The zipper is attached with fine thread; consequently, the zipper on the first bag came loose, and I had to exchange for a new bag.Opening for head is a bit large; could cover babies face. Therefore, I wouldn't use with small infant.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Not much to this product, just a clip on a strap.  The clip is secure and it's well-made overall, but I wish it was easier to put a pacifier on.  To do so, you have to unclip it from whatever it is attached to (e.g. carseat, diaper bag) and feed the end with the loop through the pacifier handle, then fiddle with the clip to get it through the loop.  Just a bit annoying, but otherwise does what it's supposed to.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: We have been cloth diapering our son for two years and enduring his screams and squirms every time we cleaned him with a wet cloth wipe that wasn't warm. We didn't want to waste water waiting for the hot stuff to come out. We considered filling a thermos with hot water, but the extra added step of getting one ready at all times seemed like such a hassle (plus opening the thermos and pouring water while trying to keep a squirmy toddler from escaping froim the changing table did not appeal to us). When I found out I was expecting #2, I took this for a whirl. It was about $24 and came with 5 free cloth wipes. Diaper changes are so much more pleasant now because we just open the little box and the warm wet wipe is available right away - and baby is so much happier.  He enjoys getting cleaned now. We also use the warm wipes on his face and hands. We have been using this contraption with the included wipes plus 12 Grovia reusable wipes for about 2 months now (plus the cloth wipes we had been using before) and will probably have to replace the pad in about another month, but we don'd mind that minimal expense ($8 for three pads). If the thing doesn't conk out, I can see using this for my own face as well.EDIT: heating element died in May 2014 - after 13 months of use (right after warranty expired). Prince Lionheart was great - I emailed them asking how to fix the heating element and they sent me a form to explain the problem. I emailed it back, and they are sending me a new warmer. Nice that they back up their product.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: my baby is 8 weeks old and has been sleeping in her crib since day one. However, even though she is just 10 lb now, the mattress sinks down a bit when she is sleeping...I dont know how good it will hold as she gets bigger.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Every baby is different but ours did not do well with the miracle blanket.  People had told me how great it was, so I was willing to spend the money, since it is pretty expensive, but in the end it was a waste.  We ordered it because he had started breaking out of the velcro swaddles at night, and waking himself up.  But, not only was the miracle blanket very tricky to put on, but he broke out of it even more frequently than the velcro swaddle.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I exclusivley breasfeed so I use this almost daily. I love the look and feel of this however, you cant see the baby so you have to hold it out away from you in order to see and it also gets really hot underneath for them. ive even used it as a shaw when i was cold thats how warm it can make you. it is pretty and can be used for a scarf but, i should have bought the other one that looks more like an apron just because i could have been able to see my daughter.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I ordered this for my Graco Pack N Play, but after putting it on I realized I wouldn't be able to sleep b/c I'd worry about whether my son would smother in it!  I'm super paranoid about SIDS and don't keep anything in his sleeping space.  This was just a bit too thick/soft for me to be comfortable with.  However I ended up keeping it because I figured I could always use another waterproof pad.  I put it on the floor sometimes and let him practice tummy time/rolling over.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: This is my 9-month old's favorite chew-toy, and it's fun to teach him "Say eeeee" and brush his little teeth with it (yes, a 9-month old with 8 teeth!). He enjoys the soft feeling of the nubby bristles in his mouth and it has been sturdy enough to withstand his chewing.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I have not tried this yet with the batteries but it was very easy to assemble. Also does not take up much space.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Impression material does not work. Would not keep the impression, it springs back. The frame is nice but not worth it if can't get an impression.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: DONT waste your money1- they are WAY too small for any nursing mom2- they are too stiff and won't sit right in any bra3- they are itchy, rough and uncomfortable4- they don't absorb more than a teaspoon before you need a changeI would give these pads a zero if I could!

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: the bottle look smokey pink and cheap! not only that, it leaks all the time and the nipple suction is poor!!! have to take it off my daughters mouth to get air and for her to get milk again,

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: It keeps the smell of dirty diapers from stinking up the house. With the twisting technology - each dirty diaper inserted is sealed away as soon as the lid closes, sealing away the smell. There is an Arm & Hammer cartridge located on the lid that releases a small amount of baking soda each time the lid is closed. It also serves as an absorbent for odor. This means that every time you insert a dirty diaper you are NOT releasing the smell of soiled diapers into the room. AWESOME!It is easy for ME to use and it is NOT easy for my toddler to dump toys into. With our last diaper pail my tots thought it was really funny to toss pacifiers, keys, and toys in with the dirty diapers. Let me just say mom was NOT amused. The Arm & Hammer Diaper Pail by Munchkin requires the user to push the handle and then pull up, a task my children have not conquered yet!The Arm & Hammer cartridge is easy to refill. This is great for two reasons, 1 being that ease of use is important to me and 2 being that I don't have to purchase a ton of extra cartridges for my diaper pail to remain odorless.This is easy to use one handed.The Arm & Hammer Diaper Pail refill bags hold about 25 dirty diapers and is really easy to change out. This is pretty accurate. I only had to refill my diaper pail once a week and the process of removing the bag of dirty diapers and placing a refill in the pail was super easy!Something to be aware of with the Arm & Hammer Diaper Pail by Munchkin is the fact that the diapers have to be pushed through the opening. This is not the most pleasant experience when dealing with a heavily soiled and somewhat leaky diaper. Another thing to be aware of is the fact that this diaper pail requires special diaper pail refill bags. This was an adjustment for me since our old diaper pail took standard trash bags, but one that was worth it for us.I was given an Arm & Hammer diaper Pail by munchkin to review for my blog at Mom Mart. Opinions expressed are 100% my own.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: My baby has loved this from day one. At 5 months old she still likes to sit in it and play with the toys and look at the lights.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Every baby will lose his paci...and once they hit the floor you are done with it. This clip works great and it is super cute! Great quality!

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I've been in search of the perfect sippy cup and I think I found it.  Where do I start?  They have a textured outside and sturdy material. It's super easy to clean and dishwasher safe (top shelf). The valve is easy to remove and clean (I put it in a dishwasher basket- top shelf). The cup has a screw on cap that seals really well and surprisingly, it does not leak!  Throw it on the floor, toss it like a batton, and no leaks.  I tried drinking out if it to see how difficult it would be for my toddler and at first it didn't work (the valve slit was stuck together), after I fixed the valve slit, the sippy cup worked awesome.  It requires the child to suck to get liquid but once they've got the hang of that, you can remove the valve and let it free flow.  And by free flow, there's three small hoes on top so not too much will come out. I love that this works like its suppose to and I love that it's essentially a three stage cup.  First it's a sippy with a stopper valve, then it's a sippy cup with no valve, and finally it can be a standard cup.  Great product for a great price.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: These are great as cloth diapers. We use them at night since my son is a side-sleeper and his pocket diapers aren't meant for that so we were having leaks. These, coupled with a diaper cover and a bamboo insert over night has solved our leak issues. They seem to be smaller than I expected on my 18 pound son, though they still do fit.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I like it and I use it.  But it's so cramped inside that I don't dare put much in it for fear the nipples won't get clean enough.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I bought these cups for my 10  month old daughter.  She knows how to drink out of a straw and refuses to use a sippy cup so these are great.  The straw is short enough so that she doesn't have any trouble sipping the liquid out.  She can also hold the cup on her own which is a plus.  They are also dishwasher safe on the top rack and I have washed mine many times in the dishwasher and they have held up great.  The reason I gave it four stars instead of five is because you can't choose which colors you get.  I have a daughter and they sent me blue cups.  We still use them and they work great but I would have liked to be able to choose the color so that they didn't look so masculine.  Would definitely recommend these cups though, they are great!

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I have a lot of wetbags and I have been anal about washing and drying them as per the company's stated guidelines. Two of my IR bags have completely fallen apart after 3 washes. The inside seams just unglued themselves. These things ain't cheap. And FYI I lined dried them! I do have a message into the company, and will update this review if they do write back and fix this.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: The old lids were good the new lids don't let enough air back into the cup so after child has sucked for a few seconds the spout inverts back into the cup.  You have to remove the lid, allow air back into the cup and the spout to return to normal position and then put the lid back on.  I have no idea why the company would change this.  I compared the old with the new and it is different.  They also drip so if they are not sitting upright, they will leak water.  The good is they are BPA free and easy to hold but I would look for another brand.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I am a first time mother and a stay at home mom. Since I am not working I wanted a hand pump rather than electric for occasional night out so I got Isis. The first time I used it (1 week after giving birth), it took me 45 minutes to pump 1 1/2 oz! I thought it to be terrible! So I bought a Madela electric.But as months passed I got a little bit more experienced in breast feeding, I realized that if I knew how stimulate a "let down" that the Isis would have worked for me. It took me 45 minutes the first time I used Isis because I never had a let down. Unlike Madela which simulate a baby's sucking which then stimulated a "let down", with Isis it is up to me to get myself to "let down".Isis would work great for any mother if: 1-you pump while your baby is breastfeeding on the other breast or 2-if you know how to stimulate your breast enough to stimulate a "let down".My Isis did squeak when I used it, so beware if you plan on pumping with this in public if this sort of thing embarasses you.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: This seems like a handle pouch for a pacifier but I can only do it if it's in a plastic sandwich baggie.  The concept is convenient but just seems so unsanitary to me.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: These will keep your child warm BUT are NOT APPROVED for use with most car seats.  You should not put anything between the baby and the seat.  These are so padded that your child could be ejected from their car seat in a collision (caused by materials compressing -- same reasoning why children should never have a puffy winter coat on their child when they are strapped into the car seat.  There is a way to modify this item -- cutting out the back piece -- to make it safely usable in car seats.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: We are returning this seat. It is extremely hard to get our 16 month old in and out (rear facing) of the seat. In addition, the straps are almost impossible to tighten. I would think its detective, but several other reviewers have mentioned the same issue. It's a shame. It feels really sturdy and the baby seems very comfortable in it, but I'm concerned we're not getting the straps tight enough for it to be safe, so back it goes, and the search continues.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: This product sounds like a neat idea (especially to first time parents), but at the best it's pretty unnecessary and at the worst you'll end up with a flood in your house.  First of all, you often make more of a mess spraying the diapers over the toilet (think poo water all over the rim and bathroom floor) than if you simply swished them clean in the toilet before throwing them in the hamper.  Besides, once a child starts consuming lots of solids, the diaper contents pretty much fall right off without any hassle.Second, unless you have a flexible supply line hose to your toilet, this device won't be compatible with your system.***Third, the sprayer screws directly into your supply line and it is very, very easy to accidentally loosen the connection (just by turning the thing off) and end up with a leak that could cause water damage or a minor flood.  I speak from experience.Fourth, don't even think of using this sprayer as a bidet, as the water from the supply line is COLD (do you really want ice water spraying on your privates?).Just go low-tech and skip this product.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: the bottle leaked and you werent able to warm the bottle without the water from the warmer seeping into the bottom of the bottle into the breastmilk

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I don't know why we waited so long to get a Woombie! Our son was a month old before we bought this because he was breaking out of his swaddles and would wake up crying every time. His first night in the Woombie we got our first 6 hour stretch of sleep. He just turned 3 months and is still using it. I'm sad that we have to wean from the swaddle soon. Will definitely remember to use these from the start with the next little one.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Started to purchase all of our household Seventh Generation items from Amazon a few months ago.  Love their wipes because they are Chlorine-free with no alcohol or fragrances and gentle on skin.  Love Seventh Generation products and will continue to use them for my family.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Okay but honestly we never use this thing! We have a bee plus and hang it behind the seat. If the seat is facing you or reclined at all it's pointless. You absolutely can not access it if it's reclined fully. It's more of a hassle to put a few things in here while strolling then put them back into my bag when we are done than to just get then out of my bag under the stroller. If we do use it it's usually when taking a walk around the neighborhood for phone and keys. We also have an uppababy vista so this is not the only stroller we use. Maybe if it was our only stroller I would use it more and leave items in there.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I was planning to use these to toss used cloth diapers in when I'm on the go but the scent is so strong that I'd rather not put our cloth diapers in them. They'd be OK for disposable, but I almost feel like the scent would stay with the cloth diapers and I'd rather not chance it. I may end up using them for cleaning out the kitty litter box.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: So cozy! Gets thicker with each wash. My son is 37 pounds and almost 40 inches and he fits in the XL. We got him one with leg openings but that didn't work out.My son sleeps sooo good with these sleep sacks- everyone comments on his good sleeping and I really think it's due to him being so cozy in the sleep sack.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I ordered this 5-pk thinking that my newborn would like it because he seems to favor the Soothies the best. I chose this one because of all the great reviews but unfortunately, my baby must fall into the small group who doesn't really care for it.  The nipple is slightly smaller than a Soothie (which he still spits out, that's why I tried this one).  Overall, seems like a good pacifier if your little one is willing to take it... but be forewarned, not all babies will love it.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: When I first tried to use the bottle, my son was screaming and I quickly put the bottle together.  I apparently did it wrong and it leaked everywhere.  When my son wasn't screaming, I tried again, and found that I had put the pieces in wrong the first time.  While I can accept that in my haste the leaking was my fault, I felt strongly that I didn't want a bottle that required concentation to put together.  Then, after using it a few times, it started leaking again and that wasn't my fault.  The reason I threw the bottle out, though, was because my son was getting terrible gas from it, worse than any other brands I was using.  Their anti-colic system did not work for us.  We have had great success with playtex ventaire, playtex drop-in's, and Nuk.  Tommee Tippe was ok as was born free.  I believe at this point we have tried every bottle on the market and for us, we keep going back to the same ones.  Every baby is different and just because the MAM didn't work for us doesn't mean it won't work for someone else, or even baby number two.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I try to make all my own baby food for my daughter and this tool is super great! It's quick and easy to clean, highly recommend it!

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: In my opinion, the most innovative feature of this car seat from Tomy is the deep sides and balanced posture. My toddler happens to be a professional car seat escape artist, and is now safely ensconced within the deep seat. The buckles and hardware do seem to be smaller than other brands. We did not use or charge the iAlert system; an electronic alert for temperature simply isn't reliable enough to leave a child in car (not to mention that it is illegal to do so in many states, and rightfully so).

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: We took these to Disneyland and they worked GREAT! We didn't lose any and now use them for long walks in the stroller and in the car, keeps the risk down of losing the good water bottles. No valves to clean, which is great when you are on the go.BEWARE: fill these cups and put the lids on WITHOUT the straw in the lid or it will shoot a stream of water at your face when you snap them shut.  I just pull the straw out, hold it in my teeth while I fill it up and then stick it in the lid after it's secure -- no drips!

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: We've been working on using regular cups for a while, and while she can drink from them just fine, she can't set them down without spilling reliably yet (or walk/run with them, as she likes to do). These cups allow her to drink from any side with no spills. You "suck" on the edge of the cup to get the water out. We haven't had any spills or cracks or anything even though it's been thrown from the high chair many times. Highly recommend!

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: We bought this video monitor for our son and used it for about 8 months with it working just fine. Then, all of the sudden, in the middle of the night, the sound completely went out on the parent unit. My son had been standing in his crib crying and by the time we woke up and checked on him, he had fallen backwards over his blankets and stuffed animals, and cried himself to sleep...it was heart breaking! Of course, at this point it was too late to ask Babies R Us (where we purchased) for a replacement, so we contacted Motorola (NOT easy by the way, they don't make it easy to get a hold of anyone!). When I finally was able to talk to someone, I was happy to hear she would be sending me a replacement. But that happiness sure ended quickly.The new monitor arrived and we began using it and it worked just fine...for about a month. 1 MONTH!! And then the sound kept going out, it would go out for maybe 5 seconds, or even for a few minutes, and then come back on. It was ridiculous trying to fall asleep while listening to my sons white noise machine through the monitor, cutting in and out, and I was also scared it was going to go out completely like our first one had.I struggled to get a hold of Motorola again (seriously??? such a big company yet no one can ever answer the phone or email??), and was told yet again to return the broken monitor, and a replacement would be sent again. Ok, fine.Mind you, its not an easy thing for a mom of 2 babies to keep hauling these things to the post office to return these defective monitors. Not to mention I didn't receive a return label, so I had to contact them and ask for one. I finally get a hold of someone and request one, and then suddenly received 7 separate envelopes in the mail, over the course of a few weeks, all with a different return label. What in the world??? I only need one! They clearly need to get their stuff together.We just started using the 2nd replacement today. And...are you ready???? After using the camera for all of about an hour, the BRAND NEW (supposedly) camera has completely stopped being able to rotate. It will move up and down but not to side to side.Are you kidding me??? Its like they are playing a sick joke on consumers. We rely on these monitors to watch over and protect our children and this is ridiculous. Also, I have a feeling they are sending out refurbished units as their replacements, as the 2 replacements I have received have lasted MUCH less time than the new one I bought.Motorola really should be ashamed of this, and especially of how bad they are handling their customer service. Don't they realize the impact of unhappy customers?

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Just bought the product in US web site, and finally found out the original is China, what a joking for me!

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: This is a great idea! I keep it clipped to my diaper bag so I don't have to worry about finding a garbage can.  Just pull a bit gently to allow the spindle to move with ease. If you force it or are too rough, it won't turn. Plus, the bags have a light pleasant scent. You probably could fit 3 small/ medium diapers in a bag if you really needed too.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I love the Wet/Dry design but the fabric choices leave some to be desired.  The PUL layer is thin and the worst quality I have seen.  The cotton layer is somewhat see through.  It serves its purpose well enough to keep it.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I chose this sheet because I liked the colorful animal print.  However, it is also of excellent quality.  It fits the mattress securely and is smooth against my grandbaby's skin.  I am very happy with this purchase.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I hated my original Boppy because it was mushy and never got the baby up where I needed him, so when I was pregnant with my second child I decided to try the My Brest Friend (MBF) nursing pillow. I liked that the foam was firm and that the strap kept the pillow in place. I also liked that the cover was cotton and easily removed for washing. But after using the pillow for about 2 months the problems emerged.First, it is unfortunately a problem for most nursing pillows, including this one, that they need two hands to be positioned well. If you're also holding a baby while trying to put it on you'll probably spend the entire nursing session adjusting the pillow. Second, I grew to hate the back support. It was tolerable when nursing on the sofa, but really aggravating in the glider because my back couldn't touch the back of the chair. Third, that cotton cover. When my baby spit up I took the cover off and washed it, then had to wrestled it back on because in addition to the difficulty of the odd shape, the cover itself had shrunk. And the cover provided practically no protection to the foam underneath which just soaked up the milk. Yuck. Fourth, I felt like I could never get the baby close enough to me without her starting to roll into me. Maybe that's just a problem with new babies and gets better as the baby gets bigger.Boppy has just come out with a new pillow specifically for nursing that addresses many of these problems. Maybe MBF will take a cue from them and update their pillow as well.Update: I tried the new Boppy nursing pillow and it was nice, but didn't fit me well. However, the JJ Cole Paisley Pillow was just right!JJ Cole Paisley Feeding and Nursing Pillow, Ash Woodland

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I like the weight of this sleep-sack better than the fleece ones I got at my shower.  Since the baby wears PJs underneath you don't need something thicker than this unless your house is really cold at night.It washes beautifully, but my son is tall (98th percentile), and by the time he was four months, the sleep sack length didn't provide much room for movement for him. If you have a tall baby, consider sizing up.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: The pitcher is a great idea to save time while making a batch of bottles for the day, however it is too small. If you fill 32oz of water, and add powder, you need to mix very carefully in order to avoid making a mess. I spilled formula all over the counter several times. The way parts fit together also seem a little flimsy, not sure if it would last. Bought a Rubbermaid mixing pitcher instead that fits 2 quarts and is much bigger and feels sturdier.Rubbermaid Servin Saver White Mixing Pitcher 2 Qt.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Caveat: I didn't use this bag.  Returned it as soon as I received it just by looking at the capacity.The idea is great, but it certainly didn't have enough space.  The main compartment is too deep and it seemed like the bag would be way too bulgy once you put all the stuff in.  I think the bag could've been a bit wider and thicker to accommodate.  Twin+ moms, this is just not big enough for everything you need, unless you are just going out for a few hours.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: It's a pretty nice banana stroller, sturdier than others. Even though it becomes super skinny when folded it is long. The shade cover isn't big enough and the basket from under is a bit hard to reach because of the metal bars from the frame cross right in front of it.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: i like the convenience of these, but the adhesive part doesn't stay on as well as I would like. Im still trying to decide if I want to purchase them again. I've only used a few so far. But, the value of these is much better than the Pampers travel bibs.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: My son is 5 Months old and seems to really enjoy these. he does not have any teeth yet so i am not having the same problems as others with the rubber falling off. He loves to be able to hold the product so easily.  He does try to turn it around and place the wrong end of the product in his mouth so the "gaurd" is not always benificial.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: We have two Britax car seats but this didn't work with either of them. What a bummer. I'd really like to find something similar.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: Before my 3-year-old was born, I bought theBright Starts See & Play Auto Mirrorbecause I thought it was cute and didn't think that I would have this much trouble with baby mirrors for the car. For whatever reason, it just wouldn't hold steady on the center headrest in our 2011 Honda Odyssey. I even tried using other objects like foam pipe insulation to try and steady it, but nothing worked. I ended up donating it after my sister gave me her Fisher Price musical auto mirror, which is no longer available on Amazon. I can't remember if it wouldn't fit on the headrest or if it couldn't be situated properly, but I donated that, too, and just decided to live without a mirror.When I had my second baby last year, I decided I wanted a mirror. Even though I was a second-time mom, I still felt a little paranoid not being able to see him, and I found myself reaching back while driving to feel for his hand to check that he was ok when he was quiet. I also disliked not being able to see if he'd fallen asleep. I did a little research and was deciding between this mirror and a Britax one. I chose Brica over Britax due to price, reviews, and availability in a local store so that I could see it in person before I bought it. I never got a chance to install it in the Odyssey, but I recently installed it in our 2014 Volvo XC60. We are using it with a rear-facingBritax Boulevard 70-G3 Convertible Car Seat Seat, Onyxright behind the front passenger seat (so not in the middle). To install it, we put the baby in his car seat, and I sat in the driver's seat while my husband installed it.Pros:-Installation took all of about 15 seconds, and once we found a position that I liked, all my husband had to do was climb in the back of the car to tighten the mirror on the headrest. The only reason he had to do that was because our older son's car seat prevented him from accessing the middle of the back seat.-The mirror is so wide that I can easily see my baby's reflection really well both in my rear view mirror and directly from the driver's seat. My husband sat in the passenger seat and said that if he turned around and leaned over towards the driver's seat, he could see the baby's reflection, too.-The mirror is tall enough that it won't need any readjusting as my baby grows taller.-The mount is sturdy enough that the mirror doesn't shift when we hit bumps in the road.-The mount has multiple options for different types of cars, including those without center headrests.-I no longer reach back to check on my baby while driving, which is safer for everyone.Cons:-The mirror portion itself is a little cloudy (and remained so even after using a little Windex on it), but I guess that's what you get for not using actual glass due to safety. Honestly it's not even noticeable when you're sitting in the front seat looking at it. I can see my baby just fine.All in all, I'm very happy that I decided to research this out and came upon this mirror! I honestly have no real complaints!

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: PROSThis seat is reported to be very SAFE. Plus fabric is STYLISH.  It's EASY to use.  Easy to carry baby to/from car.  Easily snaps into stroller base (like Snap & Go).  And one of the deciding factors for me was that it's LIGHTER than many other infant carriers.CONSIt doesn't sit in any shopping cart baskets like Graco does, for example (though arguably that's never safe, so fine).  BABY GETS VERY HOT. On several trips in the car, baby has gotten so hot & has cried hysterically (this is a baby that rarely cries for anything).  I've had to pull over and remove baby from car seat to calm and found that baby was extremely hot and sweaty.  And traumatized.  Not a fun experience.  Especially since then I had to strap baby back in the seat to get home.Would not buy again.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: My son was obsessed with toy story so these cups were a must for us!We have had them for 5 months. They do not leak, but the picture have totally worn off! I only handwash since we don't have a dishwasher so I am not sure if they were put in the dishwasher what would happen.Not worth the money.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: We had a plastic mat underneath our high chair for the first two years for our daughter...  It was very useful, the only downside being that it wasn't that attractive and things sometimes got under it.  All in all it was fine and we replaced it because it was getting worn out, and we thought we would get something more attractive and fun.Enter the Splashmat - we put it under the high chair and gave it a go - but we expected it to be a little tougher and thicker so that we could move a chair on it without it bunching up, and that it would stay flat.  It does not stay flat -  Every time we pull the chair in and out it bunches up and wrinkles.  Though we can straighten it out, it requires constant attention and re-laying.  Not what we needed, and we have stopped using it and folded it away to use for picnics or as a beach throw, we guess.  It's pretty and probably would be good for that, but fails as a under table mat to keep things clean.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: These bath toys aren't terrible, but they're not fantastic either. The squirt-y ones do squirt water really well, and I'm sure the sponge one would work well if I used it as a sponge, but they're a little cheaply made and not as cute as I had hoped. My son doesn't really play with them either.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: I could not live without this pillow while I was pregnant. I HATE sleeping on my back and mostly dislike sleeping on my side. I am a stomach sleeper and that ends with pregnancy! This pillow got me through it. Very supportive, doesn't lose its shape at all.

product_name: Baby Product
review_text: This was superb to start with...until it developed a fault. Picture was great, for the size of screen. Sound was stellar. We purchased this to keep an eye on our toddler and baby, who were sleeping in the same room. Since we were co sleeping with the baby (crib attached to our bed), and our toddler was sleeping in our bed, we were concerned that he might either disturb her while she was sleeping, or else try to play with her, put something (covers, pillow, etc) on her trying to "help" cover her, etc. We didn't want the safety risk involved with a toddler and baby with full access unsupervised. However, we held back from purchasing one of these initially, due to the cost.
